description: Create a content slide on global population growth with a sidebar-left layout, featuring a world map-style country comparison in the main area and explanatory bullets in a right sidebar.

header.headline: 30% of total worldwide population growth between now and 2050 is expected to take place in three countries: India, Nigeria and Pakistan
header.sub: Key countries in terms of absolute population growth/decline between 2020 and 2050 [m]

main.title: Key countries in terms of absolute population growth/decline between 2020 and 2050 [m]
main.body: World map highlighting major growth countries and selected decline countries. Emphasis is on India (+259m), Nigeria (+195m), and Pakistan (+117m) as the largest contributors to global population growth through 2050.

items.text: Although global population growth will continue to 2050, a closer look reveals strongly diverging population growth rates between countries
Nigeria is expected to almost double its population by 2050 (+195 million), Pakistan will increase by over half (+117 million), and India grows by almost 20% or 259 million people
items.sub: These countries still have high fertility rates

items.text: Other countries, such as China, Japan, Germany or Russia, face a decrease in population
items.sub: The main reasons are a low fertility rate and a large cohort of older people
